programme.title: With My Heart - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Feel the warmth and learn the five senses in this heart warming Super Simple sensory song for kids. It’s also a perfect song to celebrate all parents. I can hear with my ears. I can see with my eyes.

programme.title: Let's Count To 100 - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: We are learning to count to 100 with Finny the Shark! This is a perfect numbers song for young kids and preschoolers! Let’s count to 100. Counting’s so much fun. Let’s count to 100. We’ll start from number 1.

programme.title: Seven Steps - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Counting to seven has never been more memorable and fun! Learn numbers with this simple counting song.

programme.title: Goodbye A, Goodbye Z - Super Simple ABCs and more
programme.desc: We're saying, "Goodbye" to all of our friends in Alphabet Village. As the school day ends, take a walk through town, and bid farewell to all of the letters.

programme.title: I Like To Ride My Bicycle (Mr. Monkey) - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Let's go on a bike ride with Mr. Monkey and friends as we sing along to this Mr. Monkey version of the original Super Simple Song, "I Like To Ride My Bicycle." This is a great song to introduce during transportation themes! I like to ride my bicycle. I ride it every day. I ride it when I go to school. Or when I want to play.

programme.title: What's Your Name? - Kids Song - Finny the Shark and more
programme.desc: Finny the Shark is saying "Hello" and asking the question "What's your name?" This is a fun song to start the day at preschool! Hello, hello. What’s your name? Hello, hello. What’s your name? My name is Finny! My name is Myrtle! Nice to meet you.

programme.title: Wind The Bobbin Up - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: “Wind the bobbin up. Wind the bobbin up. Pull, pull, clap, clap, clap.” Get up and dance with this super fun version of the traditional English nursery rhyme.

programme.title: Hot Cross Buns - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Here’s one of our favorite traditional nursery rhymes about food, made super simple. Come sing along with the Chef from our “Do You Know The Muffin Man?” and “Pat-A-Cake” videos. Yum yum!

programme.title: Flying Dragon Fruit Cake - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Come forth brave warriors and watch the Bumble Nums catch some Flying Dragon Fruits for their fiery Flying Dragon Fruit Cake! But be careful! They breathe fire and are very fast!

programme.title: Carl's Big Adventure - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Carl is visited by three large, very dirty construction vehicles. But Carl’s Car Wash is not big enough to fit them! Carl has an idea! He can supersize his car wash so the vehicles can get a proper scrub-a-dub-dub. He just needs a few parts. With the help of old friends, Carl sets out on a big adventure to the darkest depths of the ocean, the highest heights in the land, and beyond! Don't miss Carl in his first mini-movie as he pushes himself to the limit in the name of vehicular cleanliness!

programme.title: Where Is Baby? - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Peekaboo! Sing along with this adorable song for kids, “Where Is Baby?” Where is baby? Where is baby? Where is baby? There he is.

programme.title: Finny's First Day Of School - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Finny is a bit nervous on the first day of school, but Myrtle the Turtle is even more anxious and literally hides her shell! Surely Myrtle will come out of her shell when their teacher, a whale named Mr. Magellan, has them draw and present their favorite things to the class! Nope. Myrtle is even more nervous than ever! Being a good friend, Finny helps Myrtle through her anxiety, and the tiny turtle finally shows her drawing to everyone.

programme.title: Baby Shark - Finny The Shark and more
programme.desc: Baby Shark, Mama Shark, Papa Shark, Grandma Shark, and Grandpa Shark are out for a family swim and Baby Shark tries and tries to catch the little fishies but his skills aren’t yet up to the task. Just when he thinks he finally has them a (very big) friend of the little fishies steps in to save the day!

programme.title: Dharma's Digger - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Oh no! While digging deep at a top secret construction site, Dhama’s digger gets splashed with smelly sludge! Everyone says that Carl's Car Wash is the place to get all cleaned up, so Dharma skedaddles over to see how Carl can help. With a quick mess assessment and a few twists of some knobs, Carl’s suds-sational car wash is ready to give Dharma’s vehicle a good scrub-a-dub! Dharma rolls out one happy customer, and ready to get back to work on the site that Carl is itching to see!

programme.title: Mr. Golden Sun - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Mister Sun, Sun, Mr. Golden Sun...please shine down on me! It’s the Mr. Sun song from Super Simple Songs!

programme.title: Finny's First Sleepover - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: It’s Finny’s first sleepover and Finny is nervous to sleep at Myrtle the Turtle’s house. Throughout the night, Finny becomes more and more comfortable and after a spooky story, Finny becomes the bravest of everyone at the sleepover! Finny can't wait to do a sleepover again – but this time, at Finny’s house!

programme.title: When I Grow Up - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: What do you want to be when you grow up? Maybe a firefighter? Or an astronaut? Or maybe a dancer? Practice talking about jobs and careers with this super simple and super fun song, “When I Grow Up”.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett delivers a huge inflatable sword to Neele, an accessory for the balloon version of the legendary dragon-fighter Siegfried she is flying with her Papa in the annual balloon festival. Fabio loves soccer but he is not interested in history. At the San Augustin Archeological Park, he accidentally drops his ball into one of the sites with many ancient statues! Jett delivers him a stretchy claw.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: When a girl named Alani notices that the sea level is rising around the island of Tuvalu she orders a huge lifeboat for all the people in her village to ride on. Jett finds himself stuck in ice in Antarctica courtesy of Golden Boy, Golden Wheels, and a giant Golden Robot. He is rescued by World Aircraft and learns that Golden Boy has caused lots of global warming problems around the planet.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: A passenger airplane bound for Italy has to make an emergency landing on World Aircraft. When Lime offers to prepare a meal for the passengers, Mano, a young boy passenger, makes a popular suggestion: pizza! Ratu and his Dad are excited about a "survival challenge" on one of Fiji's Desert Islands, but they forgot to bring their survival guide to teach them how to filter water!

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett delivers flowery mooncakes to Meilin, a young girl who wants to celebrate the Mid-Autumn Festival by leaving a gift for the Moon Lady, who is said to descend from the moon according to Chinese folklore. A girl named Emma lives in Transinne, Belgium and she asks the Super Wings if they can help her with her solar system school project.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett, Astra and Rover continue to help Emma, her Dad, and spaceship Rick see the solar system. Trouble is Golden Boy and Golden Wheels tag along! Jett delivers a claw grabber to Marcia, a little girl playing a treasure hunt game in which she searches for hidden treasure notes in the Rio Abiseo National Park in Peru. Our Super Wing helps her in her search!

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: The Super Pets encounter three pandas on a country road in Chengdu, China. They try to help get the pandas off the highway and back to the panda centre. A girl asks the Super Wings for help in locating a dolphin in the waters off Iceland. Jett and Paul deliver a sonar device that can track dolphin whistles.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: When a princess's dogs need to attend training school she worries that they're too playful to pass. Jett and Donnie deliver some special training suits to help them succeed. When the Super Pets accidentally knock down a birthday piñata at a house outside of Mexico City, Jett and Jerome deliver a huge piñata replacement for the boy's party.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: The Super Pets visit Pisa, Italy where some street cats have made a mess spilling recycling bins. Jett and a new Super Wing called Shine deliver tongs and help with the clean up. The Super Pets are in Pohang, South Korea where they meet a girl whose robot is in need of repairs. Jett and Jerome deliver a much needed repair kit and some robot dance lessons!

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: The Super Pets meet a boy in the Serengeti National Park whose ball rolls into an ostrich nest. The mother ostrich thinks the ball is one of her eggs and won't give it back. When a hippo gets stuck on a waterslide at a community swimming pool in Kenya, a boy calls on the Super Wings to deliver a hippo-sized life ring to help free it.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett delivers winter clothes to a little girl living in Egypt's Sahara Desert where a cold snap has left the desert snowy. Jett and Astra show her how to enjoy the snow. After the Super Pets accidentally knock down some Turkish rugs in an Istanbul market, Jett and Ellie deliver a magic lamp that can summon two genies.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: A boy, Kima, and his Dad are trekking through the Himalaya mountains in Nepal. The Super Wings bring an LED torch to help them. Trouble strikes when a baby yeti startles them. Vo, the Mars rover, is celebrating his birthday but misses his friend Catalina. Jett and Astra come to the rescue and promise to bring Catalina and her ginormous gift to Mars.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: For Children's Day, Wang Min's Dad has invited his son and his classmates to visit his toy factory. While cleaning up before the visit, a robot toy goes on a rampage. The Super Pets encounter an itchy lion at a sanctuary in South Africa. Jett and Astra deliver a back scratcher, but the lion's itchiness is stronger than they realized!

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: The Super Pets go into a video game arcade and play a video game called Dessert Kingdom. They are watched by a boy named Yoonho and they decide to go into the game itself. When the Super Pets unearth a woolly mammoth, a Nenets boy wants to keep it. The mammoth must be able to pull a sleigh so Jett and Dizzy deliver a mammoth size sleigh.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: When a T-Rex skeleton on display in a Copenhagen museum is brought to life, a boy orders a remote controller to summon a giant robot T-Rex that he built to help calm it. Jett and the others arrive in the Jurassic Period to return Tyranno to her proper place in time. A volcano erupts throwing fireballs everywhere and causing a dinosaur stampede.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: The Super Pets spend a day at a Pet Hotel in Dubai, United Arab Emirates. The daughter of the hotel owner gets help from the Super Wings to keep them well behaved. After the Jet Pets' Mini Base collides with a small comet, Jett and Astra rush a space scanner to a young boy and together and the three search space for the small 'Baba' comet.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: It's Jett's birthday and almost all of his friends are throwing him a big birthday party! Sky sends Donnie, Paul, and Dizzy out on missions and nobody is left at Jett's party. When the truck transporting race cars gets lost, Jett, Jerome and Dizzy show up in Monza, Italy to take part in a very special Super Wings race against Golden Boy.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: While in Thailand, Jett Pet and Paul Pet's fruit is swiped by a monkey. Thanawat asks for help getting his toys back so they create an LED ball with a tracking system. The Super Pets travel to Madrid, Spain where they help a boy secure a case for his pet tarantula to ride the train. However an accident causes his tarantula to become super-sized!

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: After eating Super Pets' trick chocolates, Jett can only move very slowly. An emergency call comes in from a young girl in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, who needs an ice maker delivered. When the Super Pets discover an erupting underwater volcano, a girl orders a megaphone to alert all the villagers fishing in the water. However, they don't listen to her warnings.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Within a baby's first year of life, new experiences can transform what might otherwise seem to be ordinary events into exciting opportunities for imaginative play. Baby Neptune exposes little ones to the wonders of water in their world -- whether they're stomping in the rain, splashing in the bathtub, playing "catch me if you can" with the tide on the beach, or "meeting" the fascinating creatures that live in rivers, lakes and oceans.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Discover sign language along with your favorite Baby Einstein Characters! Learn how to sign the names of common things from different objects found at home and outside, family, and even your very own neighborhood through repetition and narration. Real-life images of toys and objects along with gentle melodies engage viewers as they learn a variety of different signs.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Meet Cats, Dogs, Birds, Bugs and Woodland Creatures! Learn simple words through narration and repetition. Meet all sorts of creatures from domestic kittens to jungle cats, puppies, big and small birds, butterflies, lady bugs, otters and deer!! See whimsical toys and puppets.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: With the Dolittle, and other Baby programs under their belts, the tots who follow Julie Aigner-Clark's Baby Einstein series will sidle up to Baby Newton expecting a similar exploration of building blocks to brilliance, but this time there's a wrinkle or two. Animation makes its first appearance, and parents steeped in keep-it-simple culture may object to the springy "I Know My Shapes" song, a departure from classical-only video predecessors.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Ocean Explorers
programme.desc: Cal, Neptune, Opus, Dean, Tinker and others learn about the flora and fauna of the ocean through STEAM-based principles and real-life footage.

programme.title: The Sandbox
programme.desc: The Sandbox is where our favorite Baby Einstein characters gather to play, problem-solve and have fun. Each five-minute episode explores an area of curiosity relevant to our 2-4 year old audience. Since the four weathered planks of wood that make up the structure of their sandbox are no obstacles for their imaginations, the entire universe is available to them for each new adventure.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Every stage is an exciting adventure with your newborn. As babies grow, they absorb a wide variety of sights and sounds. And with so much to see and hear, infants seem to enjoy, and be most soothed by, gentle sounds such as your voice or pleasant, restful music. Treat your child -- and yourself -- to the delights of Baby Bach Musical Adventure, an award-winning program that exposes babies to the joy and majesty of classical music while presenting them with stimulating, colorful images.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As little ones grow, their innate sense of wonder grows too, creating an occasion for celebration with each new discovery. Baby Galileo Discovering the Sky introduces them to that remarkable canopy above us: the sun and moon, fluffy clouds, shimmering stars, colorful planets and whirling galaxies.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Hop aboard to see vehicles that travel by land, water and sky! See real-life images of cars, boats and planes in this on-the-go adventure that features cameos from some of your favorite Baby Einstein characters, all while listening to music from around the world.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Meet fascinating creatures from across the globe and learn to run, hop, and crawl like your favorite animal friends. Dance and sing along to discover animal names and the native habitats of the world's most unique creatures. See real-life images of lions and zebras on the African savannah, then learn animal names through repetition and narration, or simply watch your favorite animals play!

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Meet Cats, Dogs, Birds, Bugs and Woodland Creatures! Learn simple words through narration and repetition. Meet all sorts of creatures from domestic kittens to jungle cats, puppies, big and small birds, butterflies, lady bugs, otters and deer!! See whimsical toys and puppets.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As little ones' curiosity about the world blossoms, you can help sow the seeds of discovery with Baby MacDonald: A Day on the Farm. Featuring live-action images, nursery rhymes and fun-filled puppet shows, this playful, interactive program introduces little ones to the exciting sights and sounds of a real working farm.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: With the Dolittle, and other Baby programs under their belts, the tots who follow Julie Aigner-Clark's Baby Einstein series will sidle up to Baby Newton expecting a similar exploration of building blocks to brilliance, but this time there's a wrinkle or two. Animation makes its first appearance, and parents steeped in keep-it-simple culture may object to the springy "I Know My Shapes" song, a departure from classical-only video predecessors.

programme.title: Wheels on the Bus Nursery Rhymes For Children - Little Treehouse
programme.desc: Wheels on the bus is a nursery rhymes that little babies love! Come join Little Treehouse as they sing, dance in this fun kids educational video with our version of the classic wheels on the bus called the wheels on the bus and many more preschool rhymes for babies like rain rain go away, row row your boat, head shoulder knees and toes and many more

programme.title: Wheels on the Bus & More Nursery Rhymes - Little Treehouse
programme.desc: Welcome to the mystical and of the Little Treehouse! where you are take non magical nursery rhymes journey. Kids will laugh, dance, sing, and play along with our video, learning kids songs like I spy song, to market to market, animals sounds song, mary had a little lamb, row row row your boat and much, much more while enjoying our friendly characters.

programme.title: Wheels On The Garbage Truck + More Nursery Rhymes - Little Treehouse
programme.desc: Hey Kids, Little Treehouse is back with more nursery rhymes for toddlers that you will enjoy. Wheels on the Garbage Truck is here to take you on a fun ride where we sing-along, dance and have fun.Come join in this preschool adventure and enjoy it with your friends. Little Treehouse has some amazing animated learning videos and more baby rhymes collection like,Who Stole My Toy, Happy Elephant, Chew Your Food, Doctor Checkup Song, Swimming Song, Peek A Boo, Rock a Bye Baby and many more Kindergarten Songs.

programme.title: Farmees Movie Compilation
programme.desc: Farmees brings you the best of nursery rhymes and kids songs. Your kids are sure to enjoy!

programme.title: Dinobees Funny Cartoon Show for Kids
programme.desc: Welcome to #Dinobees, a funny show for kids. It revolves around a family that discovers and adopts three dinosaurs. This comedy cartoon is perfect entertainment for toddlers.Dinobees has some amazing 3d animations. Get ready to have great jungle adventures with cartoon animals and dinosaurs.This entertaining playlist is a collection of videos for children like Fire Discovery, Fruits of Labour, Magical Berries, Catch me If You can, Glue Trap, Mis Splits, I Wanna Fly, The Gophers, Baby Sitter, Frozen Lake, Bubble Trouble, Where's My Water and many more videos.

programme.title: Super Supremes Dinosaur Song & More Kids Songs
programme.desc: The kids are so excited to learn about dinosaurs. Here is a collection of our best videos.

programme.title: Super Car Royce - Back To Back
programme.desc: Watch this compilation from Super Car Royce Back To School!

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: During a mission to one of Robotnik's factories, Sally is taken prisoner by Robotnik and Snively, who construct a roboti-sized "Sally" clone, complete with Sally's personality, and mannerisms. The double tricks Sonic into rescuing "Sally".

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Sonic and the Freedom Fighters journey to Robotnik's lab in hopes of deciphering his roboti-sizer system. While underground, Sonic spots his now roboti-sized Uncle Chuck aboard a Badlands bound mine-train and devises a plan for his rescue.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Sally, Rotor, Bunnie and Antoine take off in a homemade airplane, bound for an ancient city hidden deep in the Great Mountains, while Sonic follows via his favorite transportation, his sneakers.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: While on a mission to the big city to replace Nicole's malfunctioning microchip, Sonic and Sally encounter the Guardian, a mysterious cloaked figure who guards a dormant wizard named Lazar and his computer of evil spells.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Sonic is hired into an obvious trap when a frustrated Robotnik decides to appeal to the Hedgehog's ego and announces a "race to determine the fastest on Mobius".

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: When Robotnik launches a spy-orb that will uncover the Freedom Fighter's Knothole location, Sonic and Robot launch themselves up to the orbiting spy-sphere aboard a home-made rocket powered by Robotnik's discarded booster engine.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: When Sally uncovers Robotnik's plan to seed the cloud with toxic rain, they attempt a daring mission to sabotage the dirigible-like craft being transported by land vehicles. Mission fails when Sally, Bunnie and Antoine are captured.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: Rumor has it that Queen Aleena's journals are buried in the city of Tashistan. Sonic and his siblings search the city for the scrolls.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: While lost in the wastelands of Mobius, Sonic, Manic and Sonia stumble upon an oasis called Lake Valley Resort.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: Sonia is invited to a debutante ball, but before she can be officially announced to society, she and her brothers, Manic and Sonic have an important mission.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: The Hedgehogs find out that Sonia has inherited a castle. She basks in the idea of having a castle for headquarters.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: The Hedgehogs steal historical artifacts of Mobuis from Robotnik before he can pawn them off at an auction.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: A group of Freedom Fighters is obliterated by a swarm of metallic fly-bots. When Manic goes looking for the insects, he is stung with serum.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: A speed enhancing fluid deemed Sonic Tonic is created by Robotnik to make him as fast as Sonic so that he may finally catch him.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: The Hedgehogs congregate on the Floating Island in their latest effort to find their mother. But Sleet and Dingo also have an interest in this island.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Kiki wanted to make a nice garden so she asked the Poppies to do some gardening but they planted the seeds everywhere in the garden, making an absolute mess. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to turn into a gardener.// Ling Ling is setting up a 'Thankfulness' tree at the center of the town square. He tells Rainbow Ruby that he is going to hold an event where the village friends can write notes to people that they are thankful to and hang them up on the tree.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Rainbow Village friends are on a train and they are concerned that the train won't stop. The engine is even smoking! Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a firefighter. // Paige is extremely upset. It turns out that she's been preparing an awesome fashion show but now it had to be cancelled because Gina accidentally spilled a bunch of fruit onto the stage.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Rainbow Village friends are gathered together at the lake to surf on the Rainbow wave that only comes once a year and they need some help. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a lifeguard and helps her friends safely enjoy their surfing. // Thunderbell is looking extremely worried. It turns out that his pet fish has gone missing. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a detective and finds out Thunderbell's pet fish's whereabouts.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: A welcome party for Prince Frederick is taking place in Rainbow Village, but the guest of honour isn't having any fun at all. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a party planner. // The village is a total mess from the paint that spilled from up above. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to turn into a clean-up engineer and starts cleaning with her friends.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: A Flower Festival is underway in Rainbow Village and Ling Ling has called Rainbow Ruby to help her judge the "best flower" contest. But just as Rainbow Ruby starts looking around at the flowers, Kiki's pet dog Pom Pom starts acting unusual and running around frantically, destroying the festival! // Gina and Jessy are with a huge fish that's injured by the lake. Rainbow Ruby becomes a Marine Biologist. Rainbow Ruby uses her encyclopedia to find out that the fish is actually a dolphin.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: The friends are very tired because Mr. Moon hasn't come to sing them their nightly lullaby. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become an Astronaut and flies to space to look for Mr. Moon with her friends. // In Rainbow Village, a super frantic Thunderbell is high up in the air, trapped inside a giant bubble. Thunderbell got trapped in a giant bubble in the middle of a bubble bath. In order to find out a way to bring Thunderbell back to earth, Rainbow Ruby turns into a scientist.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Gina accidentally broke Felicia's house, leaving Felicia homeless. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become an architect and builds a house that suits Felicia's demands. // Gina is extremely worried. She needs strawberries for the Rainbow Fruit Ice Cream Festival that's starting soon but the strawberries are all mixed in with other red objects.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Thunderbell is sad because he's discovered a new fruit and eaten all of it. Now there's none left and he'll never be able to eat that fruit again. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase and transforms into a farmer. // Thunderbell has a terrible tummy ache. Rainbow Ruby becomes a Microbiologist to find out why Thunderbell is sick. Rainbow Ruby uses a microscope to find a bacterium inside the cheesecake Thunderbell had but Felicia accidentally casts a spell that makes the bacterium gigantic.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ling Ling takes Rainbow Ruby to Ellie's cabin where she meets mischievous baby stars that fell from the sky. The baby stars were playing together, got separated from their mother and ended up in Rainbow Village. // Kiki is extremely upset because the jewel Prince Frederick gave her as a gift broke. The friends try to glue the broken pieces together but it doesn't work. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a jewelry designer and tries designing jewelry with the broken pieces.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: When Ruby and Choco arrive at the village, they find a very worried looking Ling Ling. Gina, Ellie, and Thunderbell went camping in Rainbow Forest but they saw a monster in the forest, got scared and had to run away. // Ruby is struggling to make a doll with her dad. Ruby and Choco get their suitcase and as Ruby swings her umbrella, there they are, in front of Rainbow fountain. Mayor Ling Ling tells her that today is the day of Princess Kiki's ballet performance.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ruby is looking at shoes that are now too small for her but she is unwilling to throw away. Ling Ling is worried about Thunderbell. His scooter broke so he is not able to carry out his work as a mail carrier. // Gina calls Rainbow Ruby because today is the day to show their appreciation to Mayor Ling Ling. It took a long time for the village friends to prepare an amazing painting for the mayor. Unfortunately, Thunderbell accidentally destroyed the painting while delivering it.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Gina, Thunderbell, and Choco try to cheer Jessy up but their efforts are of no use. While talking to Mayor Ling Ling, Rainbow Ruby realizes that today is the day that Jessy first arrived in Rainbow Village! Jessy was upset that her friends forgot such a meaningful day to her. // Ling Ling has caught a bad cold and was sneezing all over the place. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase and transforms into a doctor and cares for him.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ling Ling is worried about the play planned for tonight. There are no costumes ready for the villagers so it's hard to figure out which roles they're playing. Rainbow Ruby transforms into a costume designer and designs costumes for her friends. // It is very windy in Rainbow village and Paige, a paper doll, is flying to wherever the wind takes her. She is so light that she is blown away by the wind. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a fire fighter and rescue Paige.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ling Ling is worried that the villagers are all in low spirits. Ruby thinks of fun games to play but the villagers say that they have already played all of the games too many times. Rainbow Ruby transforms into a singer. // Ling Ling is worried because of the "Secret Present Swapping Party" that Thunderbell is preparing. Thunderbell forgot to send out the invitations. Rainbow Ruby turns into a mail carrier and delivers the invitations.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that a Rainbow Ball Tournament is about to start soon but he is worried because the field is covered with pokey pods. Thunderbell, Ellie, and Gina are trying to pick them up and clear the field but it is not easy since the pokey pods are so spikey. // Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that he lost his glasses. Then Ellie tells her she lost her needles, Gina her hat, and Thunderbell his wheels. Who could have taken away all these things?

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ling Ling is waiting for Rainbow Ruby, looking worried. It turns out that Mr. Sloth's best friend Chirpee is depressed. Chirpee wants to fly high but is struggling due to his small wings and lack of means to learn how to fly. // Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that the dress that the paper doll Paige was planning to wear to Kiki's dance party is torn. Paige very much wants to go to the party but she cannot go without a dress.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Strawberry Shortcake has always dreamed of seeing a certain rare flower, which only blooms once every ten years - and then only for a day!

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake (Classic)
programme.desc: It's the first day of spring in Strawberryland, at least it's supposed to be, but with the cold winds blowing and frost on the ground it looks as though spring isn't going to come this year. So Strawberry Shortcake and her two friends, Orange Blossom and Ginger Snap, go in search of spring. Spring is a young girl who doesn't want to do her job until Strawberry convinces her that everyone needs Spring's help and shows her that working together can make any job fun!

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: One night, as Strawberry & Friends gaze at the stars, they talk about what they want to be when they grow up.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: As Strawberry & Friends visit a Career Fair, they ponder their own futures. They all have thoughts on the jobs they would like: teacher, firefighter, doctor, etc.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Annie Oatmeal hires both Strawberry Shortcake and Angel Cake to work at her dude ranch. Things get complicated when there's a competition to decide which Ranch Hand runs the Best Bunkhouse.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Strawberry gets an unsettling message from her friend Caramel Corn, who owns a quaint little farm out in the Strawberryland countryside. Things aren't going so well for Caramel Corn: her farm animals seem to be running away.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: When Strawberry gets an urgent plea to return to Oz, she finds that there's an environmental disaster.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: It's a retelling of "Sleeping Beauty" - but our focus is on the fairy that didn't get her invitation.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: This is the story of "Rapunzel", but with a twist. Princess Rapunzel (Rainbow Sherbet) has always preferred being called Rap and liked doing things her way.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Strawberry Shortcake's childhood friend, Lime Lite, has hit it big in Berrywood, movie capital of Strawberryland. Unfortunately, she's become a bit of a diva in the process, and her self-centered behavior is about to cut her career short.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Strawberry Shortcake is planning a first birthday party for her sister Apple Dumpling, so she sets off on an adventure to find the perfect party supplies.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Strawberry Shortcake travels to magical Holidayland with Honey Pie Pony, searching for the perfect presents to give her friends.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: After an accidental wager with Purple Pieman, Strawberry sets off to prove that she can go around the world in 80 days.

programme.desc: Pete Seeger performs his version of Abiyoyo, adapted from an old African folk tale.

programme.desc: Amy Linker reads a story about a family that has to replace furniture lost in a fire.

programme.desc: Madeline Kahn reads Bea and Mr. Jones about a kindergarten student and her father.

programme.desc: LeVar journeys to Sanibel Island to witness the tides, feel the sand, and see plant life.

programme.title: Reading Rainbow
programme.desc: Ruby Dee reads a story about an old Badger who prepares his friends for his passing.

programme.title: Reading Rainbow

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Picture this! When Barney shows off his new painting, his friends are inspired to create their own art show. Discovering how to create new colors and draw shapes, Barney and his friends put on a super-dee-duper art show

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: When Baby Bop uses the caboose mailbox to deliver a special invitation to her tea party, she discovers that no one can read her handwriting. Barney and his friends find creative ways to help her learn her ABCs so she can spell her name!

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Barney's friends are playing a fun game of I Can Do where they each share something they can do. When BJ suggests having an I Can Do show, Baby Bop becomes worried. Barney shows Baby Bop that while trying something new can sometimes be scary, with help from family and friends, it can also be fun!

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Welcome to the neighborhood! Anna has just moved to the neighborhood and is missing her old friends. Barney and his friends help her discover that moving is an adventure with new places to explore and new friends to meet.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Safety first! After learning about fire safety when firefighter Bill visits the caboose, Barney and his friends are inspired to do a little safety inspection of their own. BJ's accident shows why you should only skate outside, and saying be careful is a good way to tell someone how much you love them!

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Barney and his friends have a wheel good fun day learning about cars. Barney creates a colorful, kid-size car, the dinos learn about seatbelt safety, and everyone discovers there are many different styles of cars.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Time for a check-up! Barney helps his friends discover the world of doctors and dentists as they learn about healthy habits like brushing teeth, getting checkups, eating healthy foods and getting exercise.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Barney has a tee-rific surprise for BJ and Baby Bop. Cousin Riff is moving to town! Riff amazes everyone with his awesome musical abilities. When Barney suggests that they put on a show, Riff becomes shy - he's never performed in a show before. With encouragement from Barney, Riff joins in and does what he knows best - making music!

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: BJ brings his brand-new toy plane to the park. Riff borrows the plane while BJ is off playing. Unfortunately, curiosity gets the best of Riff and he ends up taking the plane apart to see how it works. Luckily, BJ isnt mad when he finds out. Instead, he shows Riff how to put the plane back together again. Baby Bop is having a great time playing with her homemade boat. However, what she really wants is to ride in a big boat. Barney takes her to look at boats on the computer while BJ and Riff work on a big surprise for her. When Barney and Baby Bop return to the playground, they discover that BJ and Riff have built a sailing ship on the playset for Baby Bop!

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Baby Bop is sad because she lost her pet caterpillar, Monty. Barney and the others try to help her find it. Baby Bop is surprised to discover Monty inside a chrysalis. Everyone is excited to find Monty now a beautiful butterfly! Barney and Baby Bop are thrilled when BJ sets up a bug zoo in the caboose, but Riff isnt so sure he wants to be around bugs. When the park is magically transformed into a amphitheatre with performing insects, Riff discovers that bugs dont bug him after all.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Baby Bop, BJ and the children prepare for the Shapes Carnival in the park. Riff knows he will do well at the carnival because he is wearing his lucky medal. When he loses the medal, Riff is afraid he wont be able to play the games. With Barneys guidance, Riff has fun playing the games and he realizes he didnt need his lucky medal play and have fun. When it starts to rain at the park, Baby Bop joins Barney and BJ inside the caboose. Baby Bop pokes her head out to see if the rain has stopped and spies a beautiful rainbow in the sky! When the others come to see itits gone. Baby Bop is very disappointed because she liked all the pretty colors, so Barney and the others paint their own life-sized rainbow. When it starts to rain again, the homemade rainbow falls down and gets all wet. Luckily, a real rainbow takes its place!

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: An ordinary game of hide and seek takes a not-so-ordinary turn when Baby Bop claims that an elephant is hiding in the park! Barney and Riff are happy to play along, but BJ thinks that his sister may be seeing things. Fortunately, Barney takes this opportunity to help the young dinos learn about their sense of sight while having tons of fun! BJ and Riff engage in a friendly contest to see which of them can find (or make) the loudest sound in the park. The playful competition continues to escalateand along the way, the sounds of animals, musical instruments, and even fire engines are introduced.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Riff has many special qualities, but sometimes, his shyness makes it hard for him to meet new friends. When Barney introduces Riff to some of his friends in the park, its Baby Bop that ends up feeling less-than-special as the kids seem more impressed with Riff and his special talents. Barney helps Baby Bop realize that theres more to being special than the things you can do. The way you feel and the way you make others feel makes you special, too. Its a special day in the park as Barney and the little dinosaurs enjoy an Art Festival. BJ and Riff share some musical art and Baby Bop shares a painting of a very special friendBarney! As the festival comes to an end, Barney reminds his friends everywhere that no matter what kind of art they make, its always special when it comes from their hearts!

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: When some of the children in the park invite Barney to join them for a race, BJ and Baby Bop decide hell need their help. They show him several ways to get ready for the big race by warming up and stretching. By the end of the day, everyone finds out that there are many fun ways to exercise. When BJ starts to feel sick after eating too many sweets, he lies down in hopes of feeling better. He has a dream about a snack fantasyland where he eats as many sweets as he wants and ends up with a big stomachache. When he wakes up, hes happy to find out it was only a dream. When he asks for a healthy snack, his friends are happy to oblige.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: BJ and Riff find a pirate map, but in order to find the treasure, they have to be able to count. Since Baby Bop has not learned to count yet, Barney offers to help teach her so she can participate. In the end, Baby Bop learns to count and realizes that feeling good about herself is the greatest treasure in the world! Its time for a music concert in the park. As Riff works on his one-man band, the others make posters for the show. Everyone enjoys Riffs calypso rendition of a favorite song, and they all enjoy helping each other.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: The kids gather in the park for an exciting tea party they had planned, which begins with each of them showing each other the various ways they've contributed, from cookies to decorations. As the day progresses, the tea party proves to be a fun opportunity to reinforce lessons about good manners!

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: Why is there a stone giant holding a flame, high in the hills overlooking the town? How did silver help shape this Mexican town? Why are its streets and alleys so winding, and what happened to the Guanajuato River? What does the towns main church have to do with the King of Spain? Why does the towns marketplace look like a Paris train station? And its theatre look like the Paris Opera House?

programme.title: Earthrise: The First Lunar Voyage
programme.desc: On Christmas Eve 1968, one of the largest audiences in television history tuned in to an extraordinary sight: a live telecast of the moon's surface as seen from Apollo 8, the first manned space flight to leave Earth's gravitational pull and orbit the moon. The historic journey captivated people around the world.As this documentary reveals, however, the mission's success was far from assured. The Apollo 8 astronauts had just four months to prepare for the risky lunar orbit, and catastrophic failure would have brought a halt to America's goal of putting a man on the moon before the end of the decade.This film recounts the flight many consider to be NASA's most daring and important. Interviews with Apollo 8 astronauts, their wives, mission control staff, and journalists take viewers inside the high-stakes space race of the late 1960s to reveal how a bold decision by NASA administrators put a struggling Apollo program back on track and allowed America to reach the moon before the Soviets.

programme.title: Women Outward Bound
programme.desc: A young girl today might have a hard time believing there was a time when girls were not supposed to do what boys were doing. To them, it's normal to compete in sports, be strong, and get gritty while enjoying the outdoors. But that wasn't the case in the 1960s, when girls were expected to be delicate, clean and demure, while aspiring to secure a husband and keep house.Yet in 1965, these stereotypes didnt stop 24 adventurous young women from setting out to paddle canoes into the wilderness and live in the woods of Minnesota without any food or shelter, and who in the process, broke barriers.WOMEN OUTWARD BOUND profiles the first group of young women to participate in the rigorous U.S. Outward Bound wilderness school in 1965 and chronicles their experiences in the wild. It also captures how one month in the woods taught them they could do more than they ever thought possible. During their experience, the young women forged a special bond, and at a reunion 47 years later, the group reminisce about the lessons they learned and the memories they made, with some surprising revelations. They didnt realize it at the time, but they were a part of changing the course of history.The documentary takes the viewer on a journey over many decades with rare archival footage, personal diary entries, family home videos, photos and aerial footage of the challenging Boundary Waters Canoe Area Wilderness. In the end, the girls proved that they could always handle the difficult challenges given by naturethey just needed to be given the chance.

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: Why is a European Christmas Market also called Weihnachtsmarkt or a Christkindlmarkt? And what do these markets have to do with the Holy Roman Empire? Why do you find Christmas Markets mostly in German-speaking countries? What is the curious history of the Advent Wreath, of Silent Night, and a creepy Christmas character named Krampus? And is a Krampus different than a Krapfen?

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: You may not be able to live like a royal, but wouldnt it be nice to shop like one? In this episode, we explore the curious world of Royal Warrants, a special designation given to only the best shops and services, literally fit for a king (or queen, or prince or princess). Kind of like knighthood for a shop. From the royal dressmakers to tiara makers to the royal cheesemonger (yes, there is such a thing), we take you across London, inside the world of the royals.

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: Who is buried under Glasgow Cathedral and why? Who is Saint Mungo, and why is he so big? Why does Glasgow City Chambers look like a palace? How did this seaport town become a center for Scottish Enlightenment? And how did the creativity of one man, Charles Rennie Mackintosh, leave a permanent mark on this city?

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: Why did the English choose Jamestown, Yorktown and Williamsburg, Virginia as their early American settlements? Why did King William III and Queen Mary II establish a university here? What was life like for the colonists here? What crazy concoctions are to be found in a colonial apothecary?

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: After nearly 500 years, French is still the official language of this North American city. How has this UNESCO World Heritage Site retained its Frenchness after all this time? Why does the Chteau Frontenac hotel look like a French castle, and what does it have to do with the Canadian railway? Whats so curious about Rue Saint-Jean, Place Royal and Petit-Champlain?

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: Englands Great West Way follows the ancient path from London to Bristol. We take the train & get curious at stops along the way: Stonehenge, Baths masonic architecture, the Roman Baths, the S.S. Great Britain, sample some curious pub pies, learn some cockney rhyming slang, and visit some charming villages too.

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: Why is La Grande-Place so gilded? How did Brussels chocolate legacy begin? Where can you walk through the pages of a comic book? How did Flanders become such a source of Master artists?

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: Why is there a giant wall in the middle of Cardiff? Who built Cardiff Castle? And Coch Castle? Why does Cardiff have so many Victorian and Georgian arcades? What is a bara brith? And laverbread? (Seriously, what is it?). Where did Princess Diana give her first public speech in Welsh?

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: How did Manchester kick-start Railway Mania in the Industrial Age? What is Manchesters Water Palace? What is so curious about the architecture of the John Rylands and Chethams Libraries? Why is a bee the symbol of Manchester? Which pub owner shooed her patrons out with a broom? Why is the National Football Museum in Manchester?
